_meta.note: Built to the Armagh-locked standard. Clare received a bye to the Munster semi-final as 2025 Munster runners-up, lost to Kerry, then played the Tailteann Cup for the first time ever, exiting after two flat home displays. Three championship games.
_meta.verification: All scorelines and venues verified against primary match reports and official tables (Munster GAA, gaa.ie, RTE, Clare Echo, Clare Champion, HoganStand). Full scorer lists held for the Kerry semi-final and the Longford exit.

manager.name: Paul Madden
manager.note: First season in charge. Clare had reached the last three Sam Maguire series via Munster finals (beating Cork in the 2023 semi-final), but the 2026 draw paired them with Kerry in the Munster semi and a flat Tailteann Cup debut followed. Cusack Park, once a fortress, yielded just one home win across league, Munster and Tailteann in 2026.

source.snippet: Reflections and learnings, there are plenty, will be reflection on from Paul Madden's first campaign by all involved with Clare football.

league.note: Clare competed in Division 3 of the National League. Exact final position not captured here.

source.snippet: Longford ... gaining promotion to Division 3 of the National League for 2027
it was Longford that demonstrated their greater appetite to extend their championship season and progress to the last 12 of the Tailteann Cup.

results.venue: Zimmer Biomet Páirc Chíosóg (Cusack Park), Ennis

source.snippet: Scorers for Clare: Mark McInerney 0-6 (2 2pt, 1f), Aaron Griffin 0-4, Alan Sweeney 1-0, Conor Meaney 0-2, Cormac Murray and Brian McNamara 0-1 each.

results.note: Clare entered at the semi-final stage as 2025 Munster runners-up. David Clifford's goal (his 23rd in championship) and Paudie Clifford's goal put Kerry clear; Clare battled but the Kingdom always had the answers. Full scorer list. Both sides hit three two-pointers.

results.venue: Zimmer Biomet Páirc Chíosóg (Cusack Park), Ennis

scorers.note: 43rd-min goal off an Ikem Ugwueru offload, 1 two-pointer
captain on the day (Rouine ruled out, flu); 1 two-pointer

source.snippet: Off the back of a disappointing first taste of the new All-Ireland Senior B Football Championship against Offaly last Saturday by 1-17 to 1-14

results.note: Clare's first ever Tailteann Cup game, a flat home loss to a strong Offaly side (Offaly went on to the semi-finals). The defeat dropped Clare into the Round 2B knockout. Full scorer list not captured; to be filled on a verification pass.

results.venue: Zimmer Biomet Páirc Chíosóg (Cusack Park), Ennis

scorers.note: 1 two-pointer; first appearance of the season off the bench after an ACL injury
captain, 46th-minute goal that put Clare ahead

source.snippet: Scorers for Clare: Aaron Griffin (0-7, 1tp); Mark McInerney (1f, 1'45), Emmet McMahon (1tp) (0-4 each); Cillian Rouine (1-0); Dermot Coughlan, Fionn Kelleher, Alan Sweeney (0-1 each).

results.note: Elimination game and a chastening one: Clare led by two entering the final ten minutes after a Cillian Rouine goal, but Longford (a Division 4 side this season) answered every Clare surge and finished with a 1-3 burst, man-of-the-match Dylan Farrell's 62nd-minute goal and Oran Kenny's 66th-minute goal off a Clare kick-out turning it. Late two-pointers from Griffin and McMahon were consolation. Full scorer list. Both sides hit four two-pointers. Ended Clare's season at the Round 2B stage. [tp_against corrected 4->3: gaa.ie R2B report explicitly flags three Longford two-pointers (D Farrell, O Kenny, M Flynn); Reynolds 0-2 left unflagged.]

collection_meta.collected_by: Claude (research session, not yet hand-verified by Rory)
fields_not_found: Full scorer list for the Offaly Round 1 loss
collection_meta.notes: Clare's story is one of regression and disappointment: three years of Sam Maguire football via Munster finals, then a Kerry draw in the semi and a limp Tailteann Cup debut that ended in two home defeats, the second to a Division 4 Longford side. Paul Madden's first campaign produced just one home win across all competitions in 2026. The recurring names are Aaron Griffin, Mark McInerney, captain Cillian Rouine and the returning Emmet McMahon (back from an ACL injury). A clean three-game record. Editorially this is a 'fall' county rather than a feel-good one.
